summary: Genspark super agent frontend has fundamentally changed. The previous SSE endpoint (POST /api/agent/ask_proxy) was NOT observed in any network traffic. No SSE (text/event-stream) requests and no WebSocket connections were found. The response was delivered through a different mechanism — likely Nuxt.js SSR or service worker based messaging.

details.page_title_after_ask: Initial Greetings and General Conversation Opening

genspark_api_requests_found.name: recommend_next_steps_after_ask
genspark_api_requests_found.note: Only Genspark API call observed. This is a post-response recommendation call, not the main ask endpoint.

details.service_worker: Active (workbox-based, SW_CHANNEL_READY signal observed in analytics)

analysis.current_finding: The ask_proxy SSE endpoint is no longer used for super agent conversations. Possible new mechanisms: (1) Service Worker messaging via SW_CHANNEL_READY, (2) Server-side rendering with full page navigation, (3) Internal Nuxt.js data fetching that bypasses standard Network tab visibility.
analysis.implications_for_apf: The current APF generator for Genspark (which targets SSE ask_proxy responses) may no longer be effective. Need to investigate the new communication protocol before updating the warning delivery mechanism.
analysis.recommendation: Re-capture with HAR export enabled, or use fetch/XHR interception in the page to find the actual data delivery mechanism. Consider checking if Nuxt.js useAsyncData or useFetch is being used internally.
